title: The Pomodoro Technique
summary: Study in 25-minute focused intervals with 5-minute breaks.
lesson: Short bursts of focused work prevent burnout and improve retention.
tip: Set a timer for 25 minutes, study intensively, then take a 5-minute break. After 4 cycles, take a longer 15-30 minute break.

title: Spaced Repetition
summary: Review material at increasing intervals to move it into long-term memory.
lesson: Spacing out your study sessions makes information stick longer.
tip: Review new material after 1 day, 3 days, 1 week, then 2 weeks for optimal retention.

title: Active Recall
summary: Test yourself rather than just re-reading material.
lesson: Retrieving information makes you remember it better.
tip: Close your book and write down everything you remember. Check your answers and study the gaps.

title: The Feynman Technique
summary: Explain concepts in simple terms as if teaching a child.
lesson: If you can simplify complex ideas, you truly understand them.
tip: Pick a topic, explain it simply on paper, identify gaps, and refine your explanation.

title: Sleep for Learning
summary: Get 7-9 hours of sleep to consolidate memories.
lesson: Sleep is when your brain processes and stores information.
tip: Study before bed so your brain can work on the material while you sleep.

title: Cornell Note-Taking System
summary: Divide your page into notes, cues, and summary sections.
lesson: Organized notes make review and recall easier.
tip: Draw a vertical line 2-3 inches from the left. Take notes on the right, write questions on the left, summarize at the bottom.

title: Interleaving
summary: Mix different topics or problem types during study sessions.
lesson: Variety forces your brain to adapt and strengthens understanding.
tip: Instead of doing all math problems of one type, mix different types throughout your practice.

title: Teach Someone Else
summary: Explain what you learned to a friend or family member.
lesson: Teaching forces you to organize and clarify your thoughts.
tip: Study a topic, then explain it to someone without notes. Their questions will reveal gaps in your knowledge.

title: Pre-Sleep Review
lesson: Last-minute review primes your brain for overnight consolidation.
tip: Before sleeping, quickly review the main points of what you studied that day.

title: Create Mind Maps
lesson: Visual learning engages more of your brain and aids memory.
tip: Write the main idea in the center and branch out with related concepts. Use colors and images.
